axis_responsibility.Agent: Receives declared selected_adapter_ref data per row; adapter split is declared evidence, not provider availability or quality proof.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns the preset row shape, repeated work-lens declaration, step_template_ref contract, and optional step_alias as work-composition identity text.
axis_responsibility.Link: Owns gate sufficiency and Movement; fan-in-wait-all and strict-evidence remain declared gate concepts, not support-selected Movement.
axis_responsibility.Support: May materialize declared rows, normalize aliases, reject duplicate node ids, and run checker/smoke evidence without authoring route targets.

candidate_file_changes: support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py only if this worktree's active checker family is actually there; the named checker/profile wiring was not observed in that exact file during this read
checker_or_verifier_plan: Checker-first: add negative fixtures before implementation for repeated template without alias and colliding aliases, expecting ValueError with row and node_id detail.
Add mutation RED that removes or bypasses the node-id collision guard and observes the duplicate/collision fixture passing incorrectly.
Add postmortem-fleet materialize smoke using materialize_building_intent with no walk; assert eight distinct node_ids and the codex/claude selected_adapter_ref split.
Run focused checker/profile coverage for the catalog/template family and collision probes.
Run the full gate from a temp source copy only after implementation.
returned.design_summary: The prescribed design is structurally coherent: add optional row-local step_alias for graph node identity only, require every repeated step_template_ref row to carry a distinguishing alias, retain step_template_ref for template and brick resolution, and fail closed on duplicate node ids with row-specific ValueError detail. The postmortem-fleet preset should mirror recon-fleet while expanding the three QA lens kinds into codex and claude aliased rows, yielding eight graph nodes.
edge_cases: A present step_alias that slugifies to empty should reject.
Two aliases that slugify to the same value should reject.
An alias colliding with another row's unaliased template slug should reject via duplicate node_id.
One aliased and one unaliased row sharing the same step_template_ref should reject because repeated templates require aliases on all repeated rows.
Alias-aware targeting for duplicated templates is not admitted by this task; closure_transition_target_policy should not target duplicated lens templates until a separate key is admitted.
node_reroute_budgets remains keyed by step_template_ref; postmortem-fleet avoids duplicate-budget ambiguity by budgeting only inspect.
invariants: A repeated step_template_ref is admissible only when each repeated row has a non-empty step_alias after normalization.
step_alias affects node identity and labels only.
step_alias must not change step_template_ref, brick_spec_ref, required_return_shape, selected_adapter_ref, Movement, gate refs, or closure policy meaning.
Every materialized node_id must be unique.
Duplicate identity must fail loudly before graph evidence is treated as usable.
postmortem-fleet should materialize to eight distinct nodes: inspect, six lens aliases, and closure.
not_proven: No compileall, git diff --check, focused checker, mutation RED, or temp-copy full gate was run in this design-only return.
The exact current checker implementation location is not proven from support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py because the requested checker-family strings were not observed there.
C1 repeat-lane compliance was not independently verified from additional compliant presets under the read constraint.
Provider availability, provider quality, source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, Movement authority, and future Building correctness are not proven.
observed_evidence: support/operator/composition.py currently builds graph node ids from building_slug plus _materializer_step_template_slug(step_template_ref), so repeated step_template_ref rows collide at node_id unless identity input changes.

The same node_ids list feeds graph edges, fan-out/fan-in groups, terminal boundary edge, and completion_edge_ref assignment; duplicate node ids can therefore corrupt graph identity silently before an explicit guard is added.
support/operator/plan_rendering.py stores preset frontmatter as dict(fm), preserving row-local keys through the preset registry without a loader schema change.

brick/templates/presets/recon-fleet.md supplies the mirror structure: inspect, three fan-in lenses, closure, strict-evidence plus fan-in-wait-all, inspect reroute budget, closure policy, and proof_limits.
No matching brick_template_catalog_restructure or structure-template-integrity wiring was observed inside the exact declared file support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py during the focused read.

proposed_changes: In support/operator/composition.py, compute kind_slug from _composition_slug(step_alias) when step_alias is present; otherwise keep the current template slug behavior.
Before or during node construction, detect repeated step_template_ref groups and reject any repeated-template row missing step_alias.
After node id derivation, reject duplicate node ids with a ValueError naming colliding row indexes, step_template_ref values, step_alias values, and node_id.

Add brick/templates/presets/postmortem-fleet.md with inspect, code-attack-qa x2, axis-attack-qa x2, evidence-integrity x2, and closure; all six lens rows target fan_in_final_gate and use codex/claude adapter split by alias.

Extend checker coverage with duplicate-template/no-alias rejection, colliding-alias rejection, materialize smoke observing eight node ids and adapter split, and mutation RED for removing the collision guard.
relevant_current_structure: _materializer_graph_declaration resolves step_template_ref before node construction and currently derives node_id from the template slug.
_materializer_step_template_slug strips building-step-template: and slugifies the template tail.
_chain_presets_from_presets reads every presets/*.md frontmatter file and stores the full mapping, so step_alias can flow to composition as raw_step data.

closure_transition_target_policy still resolves target_step_template_ref to building_slug plus template slug; this is acceptable for the prescribed postmortem-fleet policy because it targets inspect, not a duplicated lens template.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No Agent Object, prompt, hook, discipline, or tool-policy changes.
No project evidence/status writes in this design step.
No scheduler, queue, retry, provider runtime, dependency, pyproject, uv.lock, .git, or AGENTS.md changes.
Template and brick resolution remain owned by step_template_ref and brick_spec_ref, not by step_alias.
